title: Buttery Light Peruvian Tuna Poke
description: A fresh, buttery poke bowl with sushi rice, raw tuna, and a tandoor‑infused sauce.
instructions: Rinse the sushi rice under cold water until the water runs clear.
Cook the rice with 1 ½ cups of water in a rice cooker or on the stovetop; let it rest for 10 minutes after cooking.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter and warm it in a tandoor to infuse a subtle smoky flavor.
Whisk the melted butter with lime juice, soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until smooth.
Place the tuna cubes in a shallow bowl and pour half of the buttery sauce over them; gently toss and let marinate for 5 minutes.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork, then fold in a tablespoon of the buttery sauce to coat lightly.
Divide the rice between two bowls; top each with the marinated tuna.
Scatter the diced cucumber and chopped fresh herbs over the tuna.
Drizzle the remaining buttery sauce over the bowl and give a final gentle toss.
Serve immediately with lime wedges on the side; chill for 10 minutes if a cooler bowl is preferred.
